_readme: Map patches: small, typed fixes applied as the FINAL world-gen step. Each entry: {id, why, ops:[...]}. Ops: {op:'terrain', rect:[x0,y0,x1,y1]|cells:[[x,y]..], to:'GRASS'} | {op:'trail', from:[x,y], to:[x,y]} | {op:'remove_objects', rect:[x0,y0,x1,y1], types:['decor','resource','structure']} | {op:'object', x,y, label, color, examine} | {op:'decor', x,y, color, size, shape}. Terrain names must exist in worldData T. See docs/MAP_DESIGN_PASS.md.
_notes: HARD/INTENTIONAL TAIL (2026-07-03, after the automated spiral sweep took defects 396->13). The remaining 13 are deliberately left — forcing them risks worse artifacts:

sealed_obj x8 — the dense NW Willow-bank treeline (x71-78 y135-198 + one at 148,273). These unreachable trees sit 2-3 deep; pulling any one exposes a fresh sliver behind it (verified: removing all 8 ADDED 4 dead_pockets). Leave them as bank scenery; they are visible, just not choppable.

dead_pocket x5 — (585,293) a 12-tile grass sliver boxed by cliff+shallow-water (no clean ford); (457,442) a 2-tile floor fragment; (692,554) a 42-tile SAND sandbar island in a lake; (766,998)+(772,999) the SE-corner landmass. The sandbar + SE corner are intentional water features (islands read as islands, not bugs). All five have NO POI/content, so nothing is stranded.
If a future pass wants these to zero: bridge the sandbar/SE corner as reachable island content, and fill the two slivers to match neighbours. Not worth premium tokens.

patches.why: Unreachable 1-9 tile walkable slivers in the west mountain shoulder (defect scanner: dead_pocket around 79-86,203-239). Filled to rock — they were never meant to be stood on.

MACRO connectivity (owner-approved): the 7642-tile north 'Goblin Wilds' was stranded from spawn (audit_world warning). Its whole region touches reachable ground across ONE shallow-water tile at (74,149) — the natural narrowest crossing, flanked by deep water. A footbridge/ford here reclaims all ~7643 tiles, giving the Wilds its entrance and un-stranding the sealed_room + treeline slivers for local cleanup. trail lays dirt approaches + a bridge (runs before the texture pass, so the ford tile is still base WATER and bridges cleanly).

MACRO connectivity (owner-approved): six more wilderness regions were fully stranded from spawn, each cut off by a single narrow river/swamp channel. Each ford is the region's NARROWEST crossing (water-only, no rock carved) so it reads as a natural footbridge/ford, not a gash. Reclaims ~7045 tiles (2815+1282+1187+786+551+424). The 424 ford at (70,107) also clears c0,r1's dead_pocket (67,105). Skipped: a 225-tile SE-corner islet at (995,99x) that needs a 4-tile span to the map edge — left for review (may be an intentional offshore feature).
Center chunk c5,r5 (first stop on the spiral crawl): a single grass tile at (570,591) ringed by 4 trees, unreachable. Remove the west tree so it connects to the reachable ground at (568,591).

c1,r3: three hollow diamond rings of stray WALL tiles (no label, no floor, no structure = generation artifacts, not authored ruins), each flagged as ~8 wall_orphans enclosing a 5-tile dead_pocket. Convert each ring footprint to GRASS — clears the orphan walls and opens the enclosed pocket at once. Rects contain only wall/grass/decor (no water/rock/POI), so the fill is safe.

c0,r2 (Goblin Wilds mountain edge): 8 dead_pockets + 43 sealed trees. The pockets are tree-walled grass courtyards pinned between the crag and the Willow river; a Charred Homestead and a Dropped Coin Purse were sealed inside/on the rock. Fix = open the treelines so each courtyard (and its POI) becomes reachable, clear the ~20 trees stranded on the bare rock shoulder, and cut a dirt doorstep to the coin purse the earlier rock-fill had buried. Removal-only + one un-rock cell, so no new slivers/orphans possible.
Spiral crawl c6,r5: clear 0 orphan wall(s) + connect 3/4 tree/wall-sealed pocket(s). Auto-solved (object-removal + wall->grass only; no rock/water carved).

Two orphan-wall tiles at (548,296),(550,296) sat in open water; the sweep grassed them, leaving grass-in-water speckles. Set to WATER to blend into the shallows. Also clears a decor left on (548,296).

Final spiral-tail cleanup: ford 2 water-cut grass pockets (504,3 north-edge meadow; 682,117 bridge-pocket) and grass a WALL-ring pocket at (408,106). Remaining defects are the documented hard/intentional tail (see _notes): the dense NW Willow-bank treeline sealed trees, a rock+water-locked sliver at (585,293), a stray floor fragment at (457,442), the 42-tile sandbar island at (692,554), and the SE-corner landmass.

Human-coherence: dissolve 93 stray WALL tiles — WHOLE components only (<=6 tiles, no building floor, not part of a labelled ruin/crypt/camp), so no fragment is ever left behind. Each becomes its surroundings: WATER in lakes, ROCK against mountains, GRASS in open reachable ground (reach-guarded = no new pockets). Objects on tiles turned to water/rock are removed to avoid clipping. Grass keeps its tree (natural). Also grasses isolated meadow-cliffs.

Minimal POI build-out (folded into the coherence pass): the lone BARE Collapsed Watchtower at (307,635) stood as a naked marker in a willow grove while its 13 siblings are full ruins. Dressed it with tumbled mossy stone rubble (decor only — no walls, so no willow clipping / no sealed room), so it reads as a collapsed, overgrown tower. Full structural build-out of bare/thin landmarks is the dedicated pass — see scripts/poi_inventory.mjs.

POI build-out (folded in, owner approved): enriched 28 THIN structural landmark scenes (Tents, Collapsed Watchtowers, Pilgrim's Rests, Charred Homesteads, Hunter's Caches, Looted/Wrecked Carts) with 172 decor pieces drawn from each TYPE's own rich-sibling palette, placed on empty grass so they read as built-up camps/ruins like their richer siblings. Decor-only (no walls) = no clipping / no sealed rooms. Loot markers + animal dens deliberately left small.

POI build-out (folded in, owner approved): enriched 22 THIN structural landmark scenes (Tents, Collapsed Watchtowers, Pilgrim's Rests, Charred Homesteads, Hunter's Caches, Looted/Wrecked Carts) with 121 decor pieces drawn from each TYPE's own rich-sibling palette, placed on empty grass so they read as built-up camps/ruins like their richer siblings. Decor-only (no walls) = no clipping / no sealed rooms. Loot markers + animal dens deliberately left small.

patches.note: Highgate v2 — the Chief's great hall as BUILT architecture astride the ceremonial avenue. Projecting 3x3 corner bastions (walls, ladder in each core, door to the apron), a gatehouse with piers flanking the south portal, wall buttresses, a tiered elevation plinth (built mass 168 / skirt 146) so the ground falls away around it, a paved DIRT apron, and exterior dressing (iron lamps, braziers, bush hedges, paddock fence, supply yard of carts/barrels/crates, a well). Interior throne dais + mead hall + war hall kept. Bank at its exact tile. Probed geometry; reachability + coherence simulated.

Plunder Market — the bazaar on the ceremonial axis south of Highgate. Cobbled plaza around the kept Grand Exchange + spawn + canal/road crossing + farm nodes; a stall bazaar (varied awnings/goods) ringing the square + a market lane down from Highgate’s gate; a central Market Cross monument on a stepped base; a BUILT Merchants’ Guildhall on a low plinth (walls, door to the plaza, guildmaster desk/coffer/ledger, banners) east of the square; Grand Exchange forecourt dressed with sacks/crates/barrels/awning; iron lamps, market barrels, produce carts, bunting. Patch-only; reachability + coherence simulated.

Guild Court vignette — recompose the guildhall forecourt as a COMPOSED SCENE (pattern-language method, not scattered props): a clear paved walk from the plaza to the door (threshold kept clear — no lamp-in-the-doorway), door framed by planters, an offset entrance lamp, flowerbeds lining the path, and a low-hedge-enclosed green garden pocket with a bench that FACES a fountain, plus setback barrel/crate leaning on the guild wall. Applied after city-plunder-market to supersede its scattered court. Reachability + coherence simulated.

Market Square vignette — recompose the Market Cross into a composed civic square (pattern-language): a stone-flagged base with the cross as centerpiece, benches gathered facing it, a town well as second focal + water source, planters and flowerbeds framing the edges, a proclamation board by the cross, and radiating cobble paths. Applied after the market to supersede the scattered centre. Reachability + coherence simulated.

Chunk c4r5 — South Gate Road & river crossing. The King's Road S leaves Gorkholm and crosses the diagonal river on a stone BRIDGE (auto-bridged only where road meets water; river preserved), then runs the east bank south (paved near town -> dirt lane) to hand off toward the mire at c4r6, skirting the SE bog pond. A bridgefoot riverside waystation (well/notice/bench/lamp/wildflowers); a bridge-head signpost + lamps; the Ruined Cottage reframed as an abandoned riverside homestead; reeds + bank-stones along the river, bog-edge reeds, undergrowth treeline, milestones, roadside lamps + wildflowers. All gather nodes/mobs preserved; every prop guarded onto dry land. Layered build; reachability + coherence simulated.

Chunk c4r6 — Mire Causeway. The King's Road S crosses the top-of-chunk bog on a boardwalk CAUSEWAY (auto-bridged over the water; bog + E-W river preserved), then a dirt road curves SW past wayside ruins to a dressed PILGRIM'S REST waystation (wayshrine/fire/bench/lantern/notice) at the mire's edge, handing the road off to c4r7. Causeway lanterns, bog reeds + mud verges, forager stub to the onion/mushroom cluster, milestones, mire-edge scrub. All nodes/mobs preserved; props guarded onto dry land; reachability + coherence simulated.

West Road — Gorkholm's west gate (Old Boundary Stone) to the Mossreach Woodcut Camp (Woodcutter Splinter). A 2-wide dirt lane auto-bridged over the mid-corridor water body, tying into the existing camp road; a west-gate marker (boundary lamp + notice), a bridge waystation (ford well/notice/lamp/wildflowers) at the crossing, a Mossreach camp signpost, roadside lamps/milestones/wildflowers + treeline verges. Spans c3r4+c2r4. All nodes/NPCs preserved; props land-guarded; reachability + coherence simulated.

East + Lake Roads — the last two primary spokes. LAKE ROAD: town east gate -> Blackmoor Landing (Bait-Seller Gribble), dressed as a fishing wharf (landing notice, fish barrel, crate, dock lamp, bench, shore reeds). EAST ROAD: town NE, skirting the lake's NORTH shore (auto-bridged) to the Thornhaunt Hunter Lodge (Huntsman Bracken), with a mid-route hunter waystation + a Thornhaunt gate signpost. Roadside lamps + milestones on both. All nodes/NPCs preserved; props land-guarded; reachability + coherence simulated.

Mossreach regional identity — turn the bare woodcut camp into a LOGGING SETTLEMENT: a widened dirt log-yard, a Timber Sawmill + stacked-timber landing by the West Road (the region anchor), a Timber Lodge cabin, a cut-over clearing of stumps, split-log fences, a charcoal kiln + woodsmoke, a carved Forest Totem greeting the road, sawdust/wood-chip ground scatter. Keeps the Woodcutting Hut, Saw Pit, log piles, campfire, Woodcutter Splinter, oaks + mushroom node. Guarded (land + not on trees/structures); reachability + coherence simulated.

Blackmoor Landing regional identity — enhance the existing lakeside fishing landing (shacks, piers, Gribble, locked island-boat) into a FISHING WHARF: a planked shore apron, beached rowboats + a moored skiff (new boat prop), drying nets (new net prop), fish barrels + catch crates, a stone shore Beacon Tower (lighthouse), harbour lamps, a wharf bench facing the water, rope coils + net-float buoys + shore reeds. All on dry land/pier tiles (never water). Guarded; reachability + coherence simulated.

Thornhaunt regional identity — intensify the East-Road hunter camp (lodge, tents, fire pit, weapon rack, snare, Bracken) into a HUNTING STATION: a widened dirt clearing, antler trophies (new prop), skinning frames of drying pelts, kill-bones + a hanging carcass (new bones prop), a smokehouse + smoke fire, a rough stockade, a trapper cart, and a Hunters Totem greeting the road; feather/bone scatter. Guarded onto dry clearing; reachability + coherence simulated.

Reeking Mire regional identity — turn the witch coven (2 witch huts, Strange Garden, Hidden Cave, Mushroom Ring, Vex) into a proper WITCH-BOG: boggy MUD ground, bubbling Witch Cauldrons + a Coven Fire, Skull Wards + Bone Fence warding stakes, greenish Bog Lanterns, a Bone Totem greeting the path, a hexed cart; toadstool + will-o-wisp + dark-reed scatter. Guarded onto dry land; reachability + coherence simulated.

Cragmaw regional identity — turn the miners' camp (lodge, tents, deposit box, ore cart, coal heap, Grint, ore nodes) into a working STONE-PIT: widened dirt pit-floor, laden Ore Carts, Spoil Heaps (tailings), Cut Stone Piles, extra Coal Heaps, a Pit-Head Tower headframe over the shaft, Miner Firepits, Ore Barrels, rope-and-post Pit Fences, a broken winch cart; rubble + coal-dust scatter. Guarded onto dry ground; reachability + coherence simulated.

South Gate Crossroads — a junction-square waystation where the King's Road S crosses the E-W road leaving town. A multi-way signpost at the centre, a travellers' well with benches facing it, a wayfarer's shelter + a wayshrine, corner lamps, planters + wildflowers, flagstone corners and milestones. The N-S and E-W through-routes are kept clear (props never on the road); guarded onto dry land. Reachability + coherence simulated.

West Trailhead — a forest-gate junction marker where the West Road leaves town into dense Mossreach woods (too tree-choked for a plaza): a Mossreach trailhead signpost, a carved Forest Trail Totem, a wayfarer bench, flanking trail lamps, a woodpile (Mossreach timber), trail cairns, moss + trail-stone underfoot. Through-route kept clear; guarded around the trees. Reachability + coherence simulated.
